title: Libanotis seseloides
intro: Libanotis seseloides, also known as Mediterranean knawel or Seseli-like Libanotis, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Libanotis seseloides include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate respiratory issues, skin irritations, and other health concerns.
